notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: We could not find that page.

auth.signInDesc: Use the email and password for your account.
auth.authErrorDbDescription: Check connection and MONGODB_URI. See /api/health/db on the server.
auth.authErrorConfig: Sign-in service misconfiguration. Check AUTH_SECRET and NEXTAUTH_URL on the server.
auth.authErrorSignIn: We could not sign you in. See the details below, or try again.
auth.registerDesc: New accounts are allowed. After signing up, sign in on the next screen.
auth.accountCreatedSignIn: Account created. Sign in with the email and password you just chose.
auth.registerClosedDesc: Use the register page to create a new account.
auth.registerDbErrorDesc: The server on Vercel could not open a MongoDB connection. Set MONGODB_URI (Atlas “Drivers” URI), add Network Access in Atlas (e.g. 0.0.0.0/0), and URL-encode special characters in the password. Registration still needs the database; this message explains the failure.
auth.registerDbErrorNote: This app has no “sign up without a server” mode—users are stored in MongoDB.
auth.registerBannerDb: Server cannot reach MongoDB. Fix MONGODB_URI and Atlas network access, then try again.

metadata.title: Personal Finance
metadata.description: Track income, expenses, and projects in one place.

monthCompare.title: This month vs last (salary focus)
monthCompare.subtitle: {current} vs {previous} — salary inflows, all expenses, and net from salary (gam3eya and other types are not in the salary line).

settings.title: Notifications & email
settings.description: Control alerts and email — see delivery status and daily scheduling.
settings.deliveryDesc: Email and daily scheduling must be configured for reminder emails to reach you.
settings.emailMissingDetail: Add Resend or SMTP in Vercel environment variables (see guide below).
settings.cronReadyDetail: Vercel Cron or JOBS_SECRET is set — daily reminders run automatically.
settings.cronMissingDetail: Add vercel.json crons and CRON_SECRET (Vercel injects it) or JOBS_SECRET.
settings.noActivityYet: No activity recorded yet — open the app once to start tracking.
settings.testEmailOk: Test email sent ({provider}) — check your inbox.
settings.setupGuideTitle: To get emails working on Vercel free tier:
settings.setupGuideResend: Resend (free ~3,000/month): set RESEND_API_KEY + RESEND_FROM in Vercel.
settings.setupGuideCron: After deploy, Vercel Cron runs /api/jobs/daily once per day (Hobby plan).
settings.setupGuideToggle: Enable «Daily reminder if you haven’t opened the app» and save.
settings.reminderActive: Daily reminder is on — sent between {start}:00 and {end}:00 {tz} if no activity for 24h.
settings.rulesSectionDesc: Reminder rules and periodic digest schedule.
settings.inactivityDaysHint: If you add no income or expense within this many days — alert (and optional email).
settings.digestCadenceHint: Periodic financial digest by email (separate from the no-login reminder).
settings.emailSectionDesc: Digest emails show income vs spending for the current calendar month and key budget lines.
settings.mirrorInAppDesc: Sends a message for each automated alert (no login, budget, net drop, etc.) when your mail is set up.
settings.recurringDueDesc: Once per month, on the day you set for each active rule (e.g. internet on the 10th), you get an in-app alert; if “Mirror in-app alerts to email” is on, you get email too. Not a bank transaction — tracking only.
settings.projectPaymentRemindersDesc: Alerts for expected payment dates and pending installments (today, tomorrow, and overdue) — in-app, and email if mirror is on.
settings.digestEmailDesc: Sends a summary with spent amount and a simple remaining-balance line on your chosen schedule.
settings.criticalEmailDesc: If off, you still get in-app alerts, but 100% over budget will not be emailed (only in-app).
settings.noLogin: Daily reminder email if you haven’t opened the app
settings.noLoginDesc: If there’s no activity for 24 hours (session/sign-in or opening the dashboard), we send one friendly email during the reminder window (default 10:00–23:59 Africa/Cairo). Does not depend on «mirror alerts to email».
settings.netDrop: When net balance is lower than the previous month
settings.netDropDesc: Compares the last two completed months and emails if the newer month is worse.
settings.inactivityNudge: No new entries in N days
settings.inactivityNudgeDesc: Warns (and can email) when you have not added any income or expense in the number of days below.
settings.inAppSectionDesc: The Alerts card on the home screen lists your notifications here.
settings.activityNotif: Log changes to income, expenses, and project payouts
settings.activityNotifDesc: Adds a line when you add, edit, or delete a row (in-app only).
settings.lowNet: Alert when this month’s net is below (amount)
settings.lowNetDesc: Empty = off. For example, type 2000 to get a warning when this calendar month’s net (income + project payouts minus expenses) goes below 2000. At most once per day per warning.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.desc: Manage and categorize your income and expenses.
transactions.createExpenseDesc: One-time (spent today) or recurring (e.g. internet, subscriptions).
transactions.scheduleOnceHint: Income or expense every now and then
transactions.recurringIncomeNote: Shows under Recurring and logs each month.
transactions.recurringExpenseNote: Monthly rule — not an instant spend line.
transactions.exportExcelEmpty: Nothing to export for the current filters.

layout.jumpTo: Jump to a screen (same as bottom bar)

notif.inboxHelper: Read each line in full. The tag shows the kind. Unread have a blue dot. Mark read to clear, or use mark all read.
notif.emailNotConfigured: Email is not configured on the server — reminder emails will not arrive. See Settings for setup.
notif.soundOn: Sound when a new alert arrives
notif.desktopHelper: For alerts while this tab is in the background, allow browser notifications (use the button, then allow in the browser prompt).

home.title: Dashboard
home.description: Overview for {month} — track income, spending, and net balance in one place.
home.descriptionAllTime: All-time income, spending, and net in one place (no month filter). Use Report for a specific month.

summary.incomeHintAllTime: All income lines + all project payouts ever recorded
summary.expenseHintAllTime: One-off spend + each recurring month counted through today

insight.title: Highlights (salary-based)
insight.over: Spending is above your salary recorded for the period. Other income (e.g. gam3eya) is not part of this comparison.
insight.ok: Spending is within or below your salary for the period. Other income (e.g. gam3eya) is not part of this comparison.
insight.moneyLeft: From salary after expenses (ignores non-salary inflow like gam3eya):

smart.title: Quick insights (salary only)
smart.noSalary: No salary lines this month — quick view compares spending vs last month only. Add salary to see the full picture.
smart.leftFromSalary: This month: spent {expenses}; {left} left from your salary lines.
smart.overVsSalary: Spent {expenses} vs {salary} salary: short by {over} (salary-only comparison).
smart.spendShareOfSalary: You used about {pct}% of this month’s salary on expenses (salary only).
smart.expensesUp: This month’s spending is up about {pct}% vs the previous month.
smart.expensesDown: This month’s spending is down about {pct}% vs the previous month.
smart.salaryUpVsPrev: Salary entries are up about {pct}% vs last month.
smart.salaryDownVsPrev: Salary entries are down about {pct}% vs last month.

income.lifetimeDesc: All sources vs expenses since you started, plus the salary slice and what’s left from salary after spending.
income.lifetimeNetHint: Uses salary lines only, same rule as the dashboard “money left” card.
income.lineDesc: Salary, freelance, savings circle, and ad-hoc income in this month.
income.lineDescAll: Salary, freelance, savings circle, and ad-hoc pay — all dates.
income.newDesc1: Record salary, freelance, or other inflows. Project payouts can also be tracked on the
income.newCardD: Amounts are shown in your default currency.
income.titleHelp: Short label you will recognize: what the money is, or which month / source.
income.recurringSectionDesc: Templates that auto-generate entries by schedule.
income.recurringPayDayHelp: Day of the month the money usually arrives. In shorter months the date adjusts (e.g. 30 → Feb 28). Weekly: weekday follows the start date.
income.deleteRecurringQ: Delete this template? Past generated income lines stay; only the rule is removed.

expense.pageSub: Top: amounts for {month} only. Below: all recurring rules you’ve saved (not filtered by the selected month).
expense.pageSubAll: One-off, variable, and one-time fixed lines (all dates). Recurring: your saved rules (per-month amount when active).
expense.lineItemsDesc: Every logged line with a date (not the rules list below).
expense.thisMonthDesc: Variable, one-time fixed, and the portion of recurring rules that apply in {month}.
expense.recurringDesc: Your rule library: each row is the per-month amount while the date range is active. This list is not scoped to the selected month — use This month above for that month’s totals.
expense.savedR: Recurring rule saved. It will apply to each month in range.
expense.newDesc: Variable (daily) spend, a one-time fixed item, or a monthly recurring rule (employees, gym, internet, gam3eya, etc.).
expense.typeCardD: Choose the kind that best matches the spend.
expense.recHelp: A fixed amount is included in the chosen month and every month after it until “end” (empty end = no stop). The default *start* date is **today** — change it (e.g. the 1st of next month) if the rule should not affect this month’s total yet.
expense.recFromHelp: First day of the first month the rule should count. Defaults to **today**; set to the 1st of a future month if you need that.
expense.dueDayOfMonthHelp: Day 1–30 of the month when you want a reminder (and optional email). In shorter months it uses the last day (e.g. day 30 → Feb 28/29). Monthly report amounts are still one line per month — this is only for alerts.
expense.categoryHint: 1) Choose a broad category. 2) Enter the amount and a short reason (what you bought or why) — that reason is what you will see in lists and reports.
expense.varDayIntro: For everyday spending: choose the type of money (category), then how much, then a short line explaining what it was. Use General when nothing else fits.
expense.varReasonHelp: One short line is enough: bus to the office, lunch out, something you wanted to buy, small daily things.
expense.varReasonPh: e.g. Bus to work, coffee with a friend, phone cable…
expense.projectTagPh: Type or pick a project name
expense.projectTagHelp: Must match a project name so this spend appears in that project’s monthly balance. Add the project under Projects first, or type the exact name.

categories.shopping: Shopping, things I wanted to buy

projects.title: Projects
projects.plCardDesc: Received = project payouts in {month}. Spent = expenses linked to the same project name.
projects.allTimeDesc: Helps you see “salary” / payout per project across months.
projects.noPayouts: No project payouts in this month.
projects.allDesc: Quick list for reference and cleanup.
projects.saveOk: Project entry saved. Totals are included in monthly reports.
projects.descFreelance: Create a project by name — amount is optional if you bill at month end. Fill the rest later.
projects.addJobDesc: Project name and client. Amount can stay empty until you settle.
projects.addJobDescShort: Enter the name (and client) — amount is optional. Save and finish later.
projects.agreedAmountHint: Month-end billing? Leave empty, set the amount when you settle.
projects.costLaterHint: Costs are not required now — use «Add cost» on the card or in details.
projects.fxError: Could not load SAR→EGP rate. Try again or use EGP.
projects.pendingHint: Project is saved — income reports only include collected amounts.
projects.noJobs: No projects yet — tap New project.
projects.deleteJobQ: Delete this project and its payouts?
projects.deleteJobBlocked: Cannot delete a project with collected payments — archive it instead
projects.editJobDesc: Update name, amount, dates, or payment method.
projects.costOptionalHint: Costs are optional — use «Add cost» on the card or in details.
projects.filterShowCollectedHint: Fully paid jobs are hidden by default — turn this on to see them.
projects.installmentsPlanHint: e.g. 3 installments — each with amount and expected date. Reports count collected only.
projects.scopeSaveAndApplyTotal: Save + apply total to project price
projects.docFooterProposalClient: Quote — scope total shown even while the project is still open
projects.docFooterProposalInternal: Quote — scope total shown even while the project is still open
projects.bulkStatementMixedError: Statement PDF works for normal projects only — select matching projects
projects.pdfPrintOk: Print dialog opened — choose "Save as PDF"
projects.bulkCollectDesc: Collect {count} normal projects on one date
projects.deliveredAutoCollectHint: Marking as delivered will auto-collect the remaining balance (cash/card from project settings).
projects.cancelReasonPh: e.g. client cancelled, postponed, scope change…
projects.collectionsPageDesc: Every project with money still owed — Normal, CSS, SDK, and the rest.
projects.collectionsEmpty: Nothing pending to collect right now.
projects.collectionsFilterEmpty: No results for this project type.
projects.archiveConfirm: Archive this project? (hidden from active list)
projects.amountTbd: Amount not set yet — add it when you settle
projects.normalBillingTitle: Bundle Normal jobs into an invoice
projects.normalBillingDesc: An invoice = a job list for one client (often ~10). Pick the client → we suggest the oldest 10 → tweak if needed → create and send (or keep for yourself).
projects.invoiceNeedClient: These jobs have no client name. Edit them and add a client, then create the invoice.
projects.noClientHint: You can open this list — but add a client name before invoicing.
projects.billingLoadError: Could not load jobs. Try again.
projects.basketEmptyIn: Nothing selected — add from the other column.
projects.basketFlexibleHint: Client swapped one of the ten? Remove it on the left and add a replacement from the right.

ai.title: Tips from your numbers
ai.desc_projects: Your numbers: ready to invoice, uncollected cash, and who to prioritize.
ai.desc_transactions: Where you spend most, and simple ways to save — from your transactions.
ai.desc_reports: Income, spend, and net — a plain summary from reports.

clients.title: Clients
clients.desc: Per-client summary — each project stays separate; totals here for quick reference.
clients.empty: No clients yet — add one here or pick/type a name when creating a project.
clients.newClientHint: This name isn't in your list — you can add a phone now (optional).
clients.detailDesc: Each project is independent — use «Client projects» to filter.
clients.projectsListHint: One row per project — even ten normal stores for the same client.
clients.profileDesc: Phone, WhatsApp, and notes for quick reference.

freelanceKpi.title: Freelance overview
freelanceKpi.monthCollectedHint: Project payouts in the selected month
freelanceKpi.dueThisWeekHint: Pending installments in the next 7 days

wallet.title: Cash & card
wallet.desc: Set your current balance — income and expenses tagged cash or card update totals automatically.
wallet.openingHint: How much you had in cash and on card before tracking transactions.
wallet.updateHint: Enter how much you have right now in cash and on your card. Future income and expenses tagged cash or card will adjust these totals.
wallet.accountsDesc: Cash and card balances — edit anytime to match what you actually have.

report.title: Monthly report
report.heroDesc: Comprehensive view of your financial performance.
report.subtitle: {month} — filter, then download PDF or Excel (exports match the current view).
report.filterDesc: Search across titles and text. Use the menus to narrow expenses by category, general income by source (salary, freelance, savings circle, etc.), and project payouts by one project or all.
report.filterDescShort: Search and narrow by category, project, and income source.
report.filterCatSub: Every preset category plus any custom labels used this month.
report.filterProjSub: All payouts in the month, or one project by name.
report.filterIncSub: Salary, freelance, savings circle (جمعية), or other — does not include project payouts. Choose “All” to see every general line.
report.emailNote: Email always sends the full month (unfiltered), same as the API.
report.emailOk: If SMTP is configured, the email was sent.
report.expLinesDesc: Variable, one-time fixed, and recurring rows for the selected view.
report.incLinesDesc: General income and project payouts in this view.
report.ideas1: Recurring bills (internet, Cursor, employees, gam3eya) — you already have them as recurring fixed expenses in Add expense → Recurring. They repeat every month automatically in reports.
report.ideas2: Reminders / calendar / email nudges need a scheduler (e.g. Vercel Cron, Windows Task, or a job server) to call a send-email API on a schedule. Next step: pick 1 “due date” per item or use iCal file export. Happy to add a simple “upcoming this week” list later.
report.forecastRecurringNote: Recurring fixed expenses & recurring income for that month, plus 3-month averages for variable spend and ad-hoc income.
report.forecastBreakdown: Recurring expenses: {recExp} · Recurring income (templates): {recInc} — the rest is from your last 3 months of patterns.
report.subtitleYearly: Yearly totals for the current year.
report.allTimeSinceStart: All-time means from the first day you started using the app.
report.filterMonthOnlyHint: Category/source/project filters apply to monthly view only. Yearly and all-time show aggregate totals.
report.rangeYearlyDesc: Income, expense, and net for the current year.
report.rangeAllDesc: Income, expense, and net from app start until now.

gold.title: Gold tracking
gold.desc: Track bars and estimate live value in EGP.
gold.pricesDesc: Live prices are fetched and cached for 1 hour.
